summary: Cathy O'Neil announces an upcoming protest against HSBC organized by the Alternative Banking group, part of the Occupy Wall Street movement. The protest will feature HSBC whistleblower Everett Stern, who exposed money laundering activities at the bank. O'Neil references Matt Taibbi's Rolling Stone article about Stern and the broader issue of banks being 'too big to jail.'
key_points: Alternative Banking group organizing direct action against HSBC
Whistleblower Everett Stern joining to lend credibility and firsthand testimony
Connected to broader 'too big to jail' critique of banking accountability
money_quotes: I'm helping organize a protest against HSBC with my Alternative Banking group.
key_insight: Grassroots organizing and whistleblower testimony are tools for holding large financial institutions accountable when regulatory systems fail.
